question_structure.context: A mop is a floor-cleaning tool consisting of a mop handle and a mop head. Let the mass of the mop head be $m$, the mass of the handle be negligible, and the coefficient of kinetic friction between the mop head and the floor be a constant $\mu$. The gravitational acceleration is $g$. When a student uses the mop to clean a horizontal floor, they push the mop along the direction of the handle, which makes an angle $\theta$ with the vertical direction.
question_structure.sub_question_1: If the mop head moves at a constant speed on the floor, find the magnitude of the force pushing the mop.
question_structure.sub_question_2: There exists a critical angle $\theta$, such that if $\theta \leq \theta_{0}$, no matter how large the thrust along the direction of the rod is, it is impossible to make the mop start moving from rest. Find the tangent of this critical angle $\tan \theta_{0}$.

sub_question_1.step_1: The mop head is in equilibrium under the forces of gravity, normal force, pushing force, and friction. The student pushes the mop with a force of magnitude $F$ along the direction of the handle. Decompose the force of pushing the mop into vertical and horizontal directions. According to the equilibrium condition, in the vertical direction: $F \cos \theta + mg = N$
sub_question_1.step_3: $N$ and $f$ are the normal force and frictional force exerted by the floor on the mop, respectively. According to the friction law, $f = \mu N$
sub_question_1.step_4: Solving the system of equations yields: $F = \frac{\mu}{\sin \theta - \mu \cos \theta} mg$

sub_question_2.step_5: No matter how much force is applied along the direction of the rod, the mop cannot start moving from rest. Now, let's examine the range of the angle $\theta$ that satisfies the above equation. Note that the right-hand side of the above equation is always greater than zero, and the limit is zero when $F$ approaches infinity. $\sin \theta - \mu \cos \theta \leq 0$
sub_question_2.step_6: The angle $\theta$ that satisfies the above equation meets the condition $\theta \leq \theta_{0}$.
sub_question_2.step_7: Here, $\theta_{0}$ is the critical angle defined in the problem, i.e., when $\theta \leq \theta_{0}$.
sub_question_2.step_8: No matter how much force is applied along the direction of the mop handle, the mop cannot be pushed. The tangent of the critical angle is $\tan \theta_{0} = \mu$.

image_captions: The image depicts a mop, angled with its rod leaning towards the upper left. The mop head sits flat on the lower right and the angle between the rod and a vertical dashed line is labeled 'θ'. The text labels “Mop rod” next to the rod and “Mop head” next to the head.
